summary: Soundboard recording with a stellar acoustic set but an electric portion hampered by mix and buzzing issues.
review: The acoustic half of this show shines – rare How Long Blues, great banter and a guest jam make it a keep‑list favorite. The electric set suffers from noticeable mix problems, missing “Frozen Logger” and some thin‑sounding numbers, though highlights like China > Rider and a punchy ‘The Other One’ still stand out. Overall the tape is listenable thanks to solid soundboard capture of the acoustic side.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers gave 4‑star scores and praised the acoustic set while noting clear issues in the electric portion; recording is usable but not pristine, leading to a solid above‑average overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Electric set plagued by mix and buzzing problems, occasional dropouts and missing “Frozen Logger”; acoustic set captured cleanly with good clarity and banter.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑set show – acoustic half is tight and engaging, while the electric half is uneven due to technical flaws but still contains a few high points.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocal delivery on “El Paso” is noted as taking his time with the lyrics, adding a unique touch.
band_member_comments.Drums: "The Other One" features thick‑nice 1970‑era drums; overall drum work is solid despite electric mix issues.
